opp.name: Meta_P2P_Intake
opp.next_step: Feb 26 Update- Pushing next step to End of March - need internal time to mock up a services procurement on ANA for something like Marketig services - similar to ERBay ask, but driving on the interactions to refine the scope of the event. Feels good for next ANA / Intake release Jan 27th Updates: Need to redirect the reference request and find another path to credibiity for this team focues on Marketing Services Procurement - Our Go-Lives are in Mid Feb to Mid March and no one is referenceable yet (Per PM and PS team leads) - UATs may be useful but so far its intake without ties to eproc or sourcing
opp.ais_why:
• SF Description is unambiguous: Meta wants Zycus SIM/Merlin Intake 'for AI-driven onboarding and data cleansing to accelerate supplier activation' across the buying cycle.
• Late-Feb next step has the rep building an ANA mock-up specifically for Meta — 'need internal time to mock up a services procurement on ANA for something like Marketing services… Feels good for next ANA / Intake release'.
• No Avoma recordings tied to this opp yet — AI conviction comes from the SF narrative and rep-driven ANA/Intake roadmap, pending customer-voice confirmation on call.

items.quote: Zero buyer calls discovered via opp/account/attendee-email Avoma pulls; no call history to anchor MEDDPICC, competitive intel, or engagement assessment.
items.source: Avoma discovery (opp 006P700000Qqws9, account 0012000000kaPTmAAM, attendee bkonsam@meta.com): zero matched calls

items.quote: All MEDDPICC elements (Metrics, Economic Buyer, Decision Criteria, Decision Process, Paper Process, Pain, Champion, Competition) are gaps after 198 days in Qualified stage. No synthesis-field data, no call evidence, no documented buyer-driven process.
items.source: Synthesis SOQL Q3 (all MEDDPICC fields null) + zero Avoma calls

items.quote: Only 1 buyer-side contact role (Biseswari Konsam, Evaluator). No Decision Maker, Economic Buyer, Champion, or Executive Sponsor contact roles. Single-threading risk is critical at 198 days in stage.
AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c are all null. No signal on Meta's AI appetite or Merlin/ANA positioning strength despite Agentic AI and ANA in product scope.

Zycus blocked on reference request: go-lives mid-Feb to mid-March 2026, no referenceable customers per PM/PS leads as of 2026-01-27. UATs potentially useful but limited (intake only, no eproc/sourcing ties). Alternate credibility path not yet identified as of last update 2026-02-26.

sources: Synthesis SOQL Q3 (all value/pain fields null)
metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or financial impact documented in Salesforce fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c all empty per synthesis read). No buyer calls available to surface pain articulation. Next Step references 'services procurement on ANA for Marketing services' and 'similar to ERBay ask' but does not quantify savings, cycle time reduction, or cost avoidance. Without buyer-articulated pain or ROI, the value case is unanchored. Gap persists after 198 days in Qualified stage, which is a red flag for deal health.

champion.narrative: No champion identified. Contact Role list has no Champion designation. Biseswari Konsam (Global Supplier Enablement Manager, Evaluator) is the sole buyer-side contact role, but no evidence of champion behavior (advocacy, introductions, internal navigation, competitive blocking, or agenda-driving). No Avoma calls to assess her engagement, access to EB/DM, or willingness to coach Zycus. Next Step update 2026-02-26 (~4 months ago) implies Zycus is waiting on internal readiness (references, UAT, credibility) rather than executing a champion-driven plan, which suggests no active champion is pulling the deal forward. Single-threading plus champion gap is a deal-killing combination at 198 days in stage.

competition.narrative: Competition is unknown. Salesforce Competitors__c field is empty (ground truth: 'unknown'). Synthesis fields (Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c) are null. Next Step mentions 'similar to ERBay ask' (ERBay may be an internal Meta procurement system or a prior vendor ask, unclear) but does not name a competitive vendor or alternative. No Avoma calls to surface shortlist or multi-vendor eval. No Tasks/Events referencing competitor demos or bake-offs. Zero visibility into whether Meta is sole-sourcing Zycus, running a bake-off, or building in-house. Competitive blindness at 198 days in stage is a critical gap.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is not clearly identified or owned. No documented pain in synthesis fields (Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, Compelling_Event__c all null). Next Step alludes to 'Marketing Services Procurement' and 'similar to ERBay ask' but does not name the operational or financial pain Meta is solving, the pain owner, or the consequence of inaction. No buyer calls to quote pain articulation. Without a confirmed, quantified pain owned by a named stakeholder, the deal lacks urgency and a forcing function, which explains the 198-day stall in Qualified.

paper_process.narrative: Paper process (contracting, legal, InfoSec, procurement mechanics) is undocumented. Synthesis fields (Mandate__c, Current_Contract_Expiration__c null). No MSA, SOW, or InfoSec review mentioned. No evidence of SI/partner involvement. For a Meta-scale enterprise deal, absence of any discussion of legal/procurement mechanics after 198 days is a red flag. Next Step references 'services procurement' and 'mock up on ANA' but does not indicate Meta has shared procurement channel, approval workflow, or contracting requirements. Visibility would require buyer engagement and multi-threading beyond the single Evaluator contact.

economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer not identified. No Contact Role of Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor. Synthesis fields (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c) are empty. No Avoma calls to reveal who controls commercial/pricing conversations or budget authority for a $100k deal. Single contact role is Biseswari Konsam (Global Supplier Enablement Manager, Evaluator role), unlikely to hold budget authority for six-figure SaaS+services procurement. At 198 days in Qualified without EB identification, this is a critical MEDDPICC gap and a deal-killing risk.

decision_process.narrative: Decision process unknown. Synthesis field (What_is_the_decision_process__c) is null. No approval-chain documentation (Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c). No Avoma call sequence to map who joins when, who escalates, or what gates exist between Qualified and close. Single contact role (Evaluator) suggests this is early exploration, but 198 days in stage with no process clarity indicates either a stalled eval or a deal Zycus is not truly in. Next Step implies internal Zycus work (mock-up, find credibility path) rather than a buyer-driven process milestone, which is a concerning signal.

decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria not documented. Synthesis fields (Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c) are null. No RFP, RFI, or structured evaluation mentioned in Next Step. No buyer calls to extract use-case validation, fit criteria, or evaluation rubric. Next Step references 'mock up a services procurement on ANA' and 'refine the scope of the event' but does not state what Meta's procurement team is evaluating Zycus against or what success looks like. Without criteria, Zycus cannot position differentiation or de-risk the competition (unknown at present).

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: No champion, competition entirely unknown, and every MEDDPICC element a gap
No meetings on record; the buyer needs internal time to mock up a services-procurement use case on ANA (close pushed out 199 days)

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Competition is entirely unknown.

bullets.text: No champion, no discovery, no EB, close pushed out 6+ months

blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.
blocks.title: Customer commitment

bullets.text: Exploratory with no champion or discovery depth

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI fit signal is unknown. AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c are null. No buyer calls to surface AI appetite, Merlin interest, or agentic-AI readiness. Products in scope (Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA) suggest Zycus is positioning AI/Merlin, but no evidence Meta has requested, validated, or budgeted for AI capabilities. Next Step 2026-02-26 mentions 'next ANA/Intake release' but does not confirm Meta's AI hunger or strategic AI priority. Cannot tier without evidence.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created and qualified 2025-12-10, now 198 days in Qualified stage with no progression. Last substantive update in Next_Step__c was 2026-02-26 (~4 months ago), pushing timeline to end of March 2026 for internal Zycus work (mock-up services procurement on ANA, find credibility alternative to references). Prior update 2026-01-27 (~5 months ago) flagged reference-request blocker: go-lives mid-Feb to mid-March 2026, no referenceable customers per PM/PS leads. Last Salesforce activity 2026-06-17 (9 days ago) but no evidence of buyer engagement or deliverable execution since February. No field history stage changes, no amount changes, no close-date slips recorded. Zero Avoma calls. Deal appears stalled waiting on Zycus internal readiness rather than buyer-driven milestone.

items.name: Biseswari Konsam
items.risk: Single point of contact; no evidence of champion behavior or access to Economic Buyer/Decision Maker. Role (Supplier Enablement) suggests operational/vendor-management scope, unlikely to hold budget authority for $100k procurement. Risk of being a tire-kicker or information-gatherer without buying authority.
items.title: Global Supplier Enablement Manager

items.detail: No Economic Buyer or Decision Maker identified after 198 days in Qualified. Single-threaded to Evaluator (Biseswari Konsam, Supplier Enablement Manager) with no evidence of access to budget authority or C-level sponsor. Without EB/DM, deal cannot progress to commercial/pricing or contract stages. Risk: deal is exploratory or tire-kicker, not a qualified opportunity.

Close date 2027-02-15 (234 days out) is likely a placeholder. Deal has been in Qualified stage for 198 days with no progression to the relevant stakeholder or Shortlisted, no documented milestones, and last Next Step update 2026-02-26 (~4 months ago) pushed timeline to 'end of March 2026' (now 3 months past). No credible path from current state (zero MEDDPICC, single-threaded, no buyer calls) to close in 234 days.

Reference credibility blocker: Zycus go-lives mid-Feb to mid-March 2026, no referenceable customers for Marketing Services Procurement use case per PM/PS leads as of 2026-01-27. UATs potentially useful but limited (intake only, no eproc/sourcing ties). Buyer requested references; Zycus has not yet identified alternate credibility path as of 2026-02-26 update. Blocker persists ~4 months later with no resolution documented.

Zero buyer engagement evidence (no calls, no emails captured in Avoma, no documented meetings) after 198 days in Qualified. Last Next Step update 2026-02-26 (~4 months ago) implies Zycus is waiting on internal readiness rather than executing buyer-driven plan. Risk: deal is one-sided (Zycus pushing, buyer not engaged) or buyer has de-prioritized and Zycus is unaware.

champion_strength.summary: No champion identified. Biseswari Konsam (Global Supplier Enablement Manager, Evaluator) is sole buyer contact but no evidence of champion behavior (advocacy, internal navigation, introductions, competitive blocking). No Avoma calls to assess engagement or willingness to coach. Deal appears driven by Zycus outreach rather than buyer pull. At 198 days in Qualified without a champion, deal is at extreme risk of stalling indefinitely or fading.

If buyer declines or ghosts, strong signal to disqualify or move to long-term nurture.

items.expected_effect: Maps budget authority and decision-making power; de-risks single-threading and tire-kicker scenario; essential gate to progress deal beyond Qualified.

items.action: Deliver alternate credibility asset for Marketing Services Procurement use case.
items.trigger: Reference request blocked 2026-01-27: no referenceable customers, go-lives mid-Feb to mid-March 2026, UATs limited. Buyer requested credibility proof ~5 months ago; no resolution documented.
items.expected_effect: Unblocks credibility gap that has stalled deal for ~5 months. Alternate assets: case study (anonymized or verbally shared), UAT feedback with metrics, proof-of-concept results, or analyst validation. Delivers on buyer's explicit requirement and re-engages conversation.

items.action: Surface and quantify Meta's pain for Marketing Services Procurement.
items.trigger: No documented pain, metrics, or ROI after 198 days in Qualified. No buyer calls to anchor value case. Next Step references 'Marketing Services Procurement' but does not state operational/financial pain or urgency.
items.expected_effect: Establishes quantified value case (cost savings, cycle time, error reduction) and pain owner; creates urgency and compelling event; essential foundation for Economic Buyer engagement and business-case approval.

items.action: Map Meta's decision process and procurement timeline for this initiative.
items.trigger: Decision process, approval chain, and procurement timeline unknown after 198 days. No documented gates, milestones, or buyer-driven calendar. Close date 2027-02-15 (234 days out) appears to be placeholder.
items.expected_effect: Reveals whether Meta has allocated budget, set procurement timeline, and defined decision gates; aligns Zycus execution to buyer's process; exposes whether 2027-02-15 close is realistic or deal is exploratory/unfunded.

items: 198 days in Qualified stage with no progression to Formal Evaluation or Shortlisted (enterprise norm: 30-60 days in Qualified).
Zero buyer calls discovered via opp/account/attendee Avoma pulls; no recorded buyer engagement.
Single-threaded to Evaluator (Biseswari Konsam, Supplier Enablement Manager); no multi-threading, no EB/DM, no Champion.
Last Next Step update 2026-02-26 (~4 months ago) pushed timeline to end of March 2026 (now 3 months past) with no subsequent update or buyer milestone.
All MEDDPICC elements are gaps (no Metrics, EB, Criteria, Process, Paper, Pain, Champion, Competition documented).
No AIS score; AI positioning (Merlin, ANA) is unvalidated by buyer.
Reference request blocker identified 2026-01-27 (~5 months ago) with no resolution documented as of last update 2026-02-26.
confidence_signals.summary: Confidence signals are absent. No champion, no Economic Buyer/Decision Maker, no buyer calls, no documented pain/metrics/ROI, no competitive intel, no buyer-driven timeline, and 198 days in Qualified with no stage progression. Last substantive update 2026-02-26 (~4 months ago) pushed timeline to end of March 2026 (now past) with no follow-up. Single-threaded to an Evaluator with no evidence of buying authority. Deal exhibits classic tire-kicker or exploratory pattern rather than qualified pipeline. Cooling risk is high; without direct buyer engagement and multi-threading in next 14 days, recommend disqualify or move to long-term nurture.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2025-12-10, qualified same day (inferred), 198 days in Qualified stage as of 2026-06-26. Close date 2027-02-15 is 234 days out. Last activity 2026-06-17 (9 days ago). Next step dated 2026-02-26 (~4 months ago) pushed timeline to end of March 2026 (~3 months ago), which has passed. No forward movement since February update. Time-in-stage (198 days in Qualified) significantly exceeds enterprise norm of 30-60 days for this stage.

Reference request blocked per 2026-01-27 update: go-lives scheduled mid-Feb to mid-March 2026, no referenceable customers yet per PM/PS leads; UATs potentially useful but limited to intake without eproc/sourcing ties.
Last Salesforce activity 2026-06-17 (9 days ago) but no evidence of buyer-facing deliverable or buyer engagement since February update.
Zero buyer calls discovered via opp/account/attendee email pulls; no Avoma call history to anchor MEDDPICC, competitive position, or buyer engagement.

north_star_verdict.headline: 198 days in Qualified with zero buyer engagement since February, no decision maker identified, single-threaded to one evaluator, stalled 4 months past next-step date, complete competitive blindness, and no active deal motion. Deal appears dormant.

best_practice_check.summary: Deal is 198 days into Qualified stage with zero MEDDPICC coverage, single-threaded to an Evaluator, no buyer calls, and stalled on internal Zycus readiness (references, credibility path). Risk of being a tire-kicker or exploratory conversation rather than a qualified opportunity. Immediate need: direct buyer engagement to validate deal reality, map power (EB/DM), and establish champion.

competitive_position.summary: Competition is entirely unknown. No competitor named in any Salesforce field, Next Step, Task, Event, or Avoma call. Salesforce Competitors__c is null (ground truth: 'unknown'). Next Step 2026-02-26 references 'similar to ERBay ask' but does not clarify whether ERBay is a vendor, an internal Meta system, or a prior procurement pattern. Zero visibility into whether Meta is sole-sourcing, running a multi-vendor eval, considering incumbent/alternative, or building in-house. Competitive blindness is a critical risk: Zycus cannot position differentiation, de-risk threats, or validate sole-source status. At 198 days in Qualified with no competitive intel, this deal may be exploratory or Zycus may be unaware of a live bake-off.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is unvalidated. Products in scope include Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, and ANA (Agentic Negotiation Assistant), but no AIS score, no buyer calls to assess AI appetite, and no evidence Meta has validated Merlin/ANA fit or ROI. Next Step references 'ANA/Intake release' but does not confirm Meta is AI-forward or has signed off on agentic use cases. Without buyer validation, risk of over-positioning AI to a buyer not ready or under-positioning to an AI-hungry buyer is high.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Customer expectations and fit are unvalidated. No documented business requirements, decision criteria, or evaluation rubric. No buyer calls to assess product-market fit, AI appetite, or Merlin positioning. Products in scope (iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA) are broad but not anchored to Meta's stated pain or use case. Next Step references 'Marketing Services Procurement' focus and 'mock up services procurement on ANA' but does not confirm Meta has validated ANA (Agentic Negotiation Assistant) fit or AI readiness. Without structured fit validation, risk of misalignment or late-stage disqualification is high.

hard.ais_why:
• SF Description is unambiguous: Meta wants Zycus SIM/Merlin Intake 'for AI-driven onboarding and data cleansing to accelerate supplier activation' across the buying cycle.
• Late-Feb next step has the rep building an ANA mock-up specifically for Meta — 'need internal time to mock up a services procurement on ANA for something like Marketing services… Feels good for next ANA / Intake release'.
• No Avoma recordings tied to this opp yet — AI conviction comes from the SF narrative and rep-driven ANA/Intake roadmap, pending customer-voice confirmation on call.
hard.next_step:
Feb 26 Update- Pushing next step to End of March - need internal time to mock up a services procurement on ANA for something like Marketig services - similar to ERBay ask, but driving on the interactions to refine the scope of the event.  Feels good for next ANA / Intake release 

Jan 27th Updates:
Need to redirect the reference request and find another path to credibiity for this team focues on Marketing Services Procurement
- Our Go-Lives are in Mid Feb to Mid March and no one is referenceable yet (Per PM and PS team leads)
- UATs may be useful but so far its intake without ties to eproc or sourcing

pulse.summary: Live: last verified Salesforce activity 9 day(s) ago (2026-06-17).

rep_outreach.note: rep reached out, awaiting buyer reply

deltas.subject: Single-threaded risk (critical): Only 1 buyer contact role (Biseswari Konsam, Evaluator) after 198 days in Qualified. No Decision Maker, Economic Buyer, or Champion identified. Multi-threading is essential to de-risk ghost/tire-kicker scenario and advance deal. Immediate action: map Meta procurement org, identify budget owner and decision authority, and secure intros beyond Biseswari.

Deal stalled on Zycus internal readiness, not buyer milestone: Last update 2026-02-26 (~4 months ago) pushed timeline to end of March 2026 for internal mock-up work and alternate credibility path (references unavailable). This is a RED FLAG: a healthy deal is driven by buyer milestones (RFP, eval, proposal), not seller readiness. Suggests Zycus is chasing an opportunity that may not be real or prioritized by the relevant stakeholder action: direct buyer engagement to validate Meta's timeline, budget allocation, and procurement priority for this initiative.

Zero buyer calls after 6.5 months: No Avoma call history via opp/account/attendee pulls. At 198 days in Qualified, absence of discovery, demo, or qualification calls indicates either (a) deal is paper-only (email/form-fill, no relationship), (b) calls happened outside Avoma (unrecorded), or (c) opportunity is a placeholder. All three scenarios are high-risk. Best practice: demand a live buyer call within next 14 days to validate deal reality, map stakeholders, surface pain/metrics, and establish champion. If buyer declines or ghosts, recommend disqualify or move to nurture.

This is a RED FLAG: a healthy deal is driven by buyer milestones (RFP, eval, proposal), not seller readiness. Suggests Zycus is chasing an opportunity that may not be real or prioritized by the relevant stakeholder action: direct buyer engagement to validate Meta's timeline, budget allocation, and procurement priority for this initiative.

value.flag: Zero buyer calls after 6.5 months: No Avoma call history via opp/account/attendee pulls. At 198 days in Qualified, absence of discovery, demo, or qualification calls indicates either (a) deal is paper-only (email/form-fill, no relationship), (b) calls happened outside Avoma (unrecorded), or (c) opportunity is a placeholder. All three scenarios are high-risk. Best practice: demand a live buyer call within next 14 days to validate deal reality, map stakeholders, surface pain/metrics, and establish champion. If buyer declines or ghosts, recommend disqualify or move to nurture.
